title: White Christmas Blues

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I never watched one of these sober! I gotta get this bloodbath off my kids' show!
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about cartoon violence being censored
jokes.punchline: Admits he's never watched kids' cartoons sober and realizes how violent they are
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-awareness moment with perfect character integration - only Homer would complain about censorship while admitting he's usually drunk

jokes.text: Bart, don't use language like that. / Man, things sure have turned to crap.
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately uses the same language
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective hypocrisy joke with good economy

jokes.text: Marge, to that I say, boo, humbug.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to take down Halloween decorations before putting up Christmas ones
jokes.punchline: Homer combines 'boo' (Halloween) with 'bah, humbug' (Christmas)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining holidays with good economy

jokes.text: where the Eskimos now have a hundred words for 'nothing.'
jokes.setup: News about global warming affecting Alaska
jokes.punchline: Subverts the cliche about Eskimos having many words for snow
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of a cultural cliche with perfect economy and originality

jokes.text: By pure coincidence, every scientist was right.
jokes.setup: News about global warming predictions coming true
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically calling scientific consensus a 'coincidence'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical commentary on climate change denial with good economy

jokes.text: Oh, you know what this means, kids? I don't have to pick up the dog poops!
jokes.punchline: His main concern is avoiding poop cleanup duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - finding the most selfish benefit of snow

jokes.text: radioactive steam from the nuclear plant and, of course, tire fire particulate
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of tire fire as normal Springfield hazard
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Springfield's environmental disasters treated as normal

jokes.text: Idiots? Why do we reelect this guy? / 'Cause his opponent has a long Slavic name.
jokes.punchline: They reelect him due to xenophobia
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about voter prejudices with excellent economy

jokes.text: Stay in our hotels! Slurp our spaghetti. Die in our hospitals.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to death in hospitals as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with dark humor - 'slurp our spaghetti' to 'die in our hospitals' is perfect progression

jokes.text: then gouge the hell out of 'em! Who? Who will gouge with me?
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically recruiting citizens to price-gouge tourists
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for corrupt Mayor Quimby being openly mercenary

jokes.text: Why would a robot need mittens? / Why would a little boy need an aspirin? / I don't know.
jokes.punchline: Robot's non-sequitur response and mutual confusion
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist exchange with robot logic

jokes.text: God, I bent down to pick up a piece of candy and rolled down a hill. You always think it's the other guy that'll turn into a giant snowball. Never you.
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about snowball formation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Wiggum-esque pseudo-philosophical observation about a ridiculous situation

jokes.text: 'First in Freeways,' 'The Yuh-Huh State,' 'Jewel of the Fracking Belt'? 'Still a British Colony at Heart,' 'Land of Many Water Snakes'?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd state slogans
jokes.explanation: Excellent satirical take on state slogans with escalating absurdity - 'Land of Many Water Snakes' is inspired

jokes.text: People, I'm completely out of milk! I do have several DVDs of the movie Milk, an inspiring return to form for director Gus Van Sant.
jokes.setup: Apu announcing he's out of milk
jokes.punchline: Offers movie DVDs instead with pretentious film criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu character moment combining helpful service with intellectual pretension

jokes.text: Uh, do you have Life of Pi? / No, but I have some home movies of me on a canoe with a big dog. / People who have never seen a movie say it is a good movie.
jokes.setup: Customer asking for Life of Pi movie
jokes.punchline: Apu's ridiculous substitute and backhanded review
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from absurd substitute to devastating film criticism

jokes.text: And when you can't afford Christmas, you've failed as a family. That's what all the big stores say.
jokes.setup: Homer saying they can't afford Christmas
jokes.punchline: Attributing family failure definition to corporate marketing
jokes.explanation: Sharp critique of Christmas commercialization with Homer's naive delivery

jokes.text: Well, there's a rec room off the kitchen, but sometimes it's there and sometimes it isn't. Our house is very odd that way.
jokes.setup: Tourist family asking for a room
jokes.punchline: Marge casually mentions room's inconsistent existence
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd explanation delivered with perfect Marge matter-of-factness

jokes.text: Passive-aggressive.
jokes.setup: Tourist husband asking what word people use to describe them
jokes.punchline: Wife immediately demonstrating the behavior by correcting him
jokes.explanation: Elegant demonstration of the behavior while naming it - economical and well-structured

jokes.text: Oh, good. Marge remarried after I died. Wait a minute. I'm not dead. What's going on?!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing strangers in his house
jokes.punchline: Assumes he died rather than considering other explanations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - death is more plausible than other explanations. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't know. It won't seem like Christmas without opening up a giant credit card bill in January.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining benefits of the bed and breakfast
jokes.punchline: Homer missing Christmas debt as tradition
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Christmas commercialism with good character voice

jokes.text: W.W.B.J.D.? / What would Baby Jesus do?
jokes.setup: Marge trying to inspire Homer with acronym
jokes.punchline: Religious spin on 'What Would Jesus Do'
jokes.explanation: Mild variation on familiar acronym, functional but not particularly original

jokes.text: Well, this crazy scheme is the kind of impulsive behavior I want to encourage in you. / Mmm... I'm in! 100%! / Now, Homie, table three needs more bread. / I'm on a break.
jokes.punchline: Immediately claims break when asked to work
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer laziness - enthusiastic commitment followed by immediate avoidance

jokes.text: Welcome to our day care center. All girls, please move to cootie quarantine. / We've got a two-month-old. Are you sure you can handle her? / Just don't lose this claim ticket.
jokes.punchline: Treating babies like dry cleaning with claim tickets
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist premise with Bart's entrepreneurial incompetence

jokes.text: That church doesn't look very inspiring. What a bland marquee. It doesn't look very inviting.
jokes.punchline: Blunt criticism of church's appearance
jokes.explanation: Weak setup for Reverend Lovejoy's crisis, lacks comedic punch on its own

jokes.text: Lord, I'm just a small-town minister. We don't have Mormon money.
jokes.setup: Reverend praying for help with sermon
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing Mormon wealth disparity
jokes.explanation: Sharp religious humor with good economy

jokes.text: Oh, this is black-church good.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy writing his sermon and getting inspired
jokes.punchline: Comparing his own preaching to stereotypically dynamic style
jokes.explanation: Cultural stereotype reference, mild humor but relies on cliche

jokes.text: Ooh, I didn't know there were doves in there.
jokes.punchline: Homer's surprised observation
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag reaction, adequate but not memorable

jokes.text: When he starts with 'my friends,' it's always bad.
jokes.punchline: Audience recognizing bad sermon opening formula
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on speech conventions with excellent economy

jokes.text: Christmas didn't get to be the number one holiday by being about love.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Christmas should be about love not money
jokes.punchline: Homer treating holidays like competitive business
jokes.explanation: Sharp critique of Christmas commercialization with perfect Homer logic

jokes.text: Um, when a woman talks, she just wants to be heard. / Where's the end?
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by wanting Lisa to stop talking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer hypocrisy with good structure and character integration

jokes.text: Well, they don't grow on trees. / Uh, yeah, they do. / Oh.
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal correction and Marge's realization
jokes.explanation: Simple literal interpretation joke with good economy

jokes.text: Where do the people sit? / I thought they were just gonna watch me.
jokes.setup: Customer asking about carriage ride seating
jokes.punchline: Homer's egocentric misunderstanding of service
jokes.explanation: Good Homer self-centeredness - assumes he's the entertainment

jokes.text: The secret is: Don't read the comment cards.
jokes.punchline: Homer's willful ignorance as business strategy
jokes.explanation: Good business 'wisdom' that's quintessentially Homer

jokes.text: By the time Princess Summer comes to save you, you'll all be blood in my beard. Ho, ho, ho, ho. Your own father's beard.
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark children's show content with Santa laugh
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark parody of children's Christmas programming

jokes.text: Well, I'm down to a kiosk, thanks to that place. Actually, half a kiosk. Sharing it with Nasreen here.
jokes.punchline: Escalating business failure from store to half-kiosk
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ned's economic downfall with proper structure

jokes.text: No cream is finer than mall kiosk cream. J.Lo use it. / It is no wonder you are not married.
jokes.punchline: Brutal personal attack on potential customer
jokes.explanation: Good aggressive sales character with harsh rejection

jokes.text: Christmas carols only have one verse. Well, they may have more, but the second verse is where they get all weird and religiousy.
jokes.punchline: Complaint about religious content in Christmas songs
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Christmas secularization with good Homer voice

jokes.text: Christmas carols only have one verse. Well, they may have more, but the second verse is where they get all weird and religiousy.
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of religious content in Christmas songs
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about secularized Christmas culture with Homer's perspective

jokes.text: For your information, Gloria, my wassail is just Gatorade I put in the microwave.
jokes.punchline: Homer's lazy preparation method revealed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer corner-cutting with defensive revelation

jokes.text: It's not mistletoe, just cherries and lettuce. Oh! I thought when I ate it, it wasn't poisonous.
jokes.punchline: Guest ate it and judged safety by survival
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic about testing plant safety

jokes.text: It's not mistletoe, just cherries and lettuce.
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre mistletoe substitute
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual substitution that's both random and somehow logical for Homer

jokes.text: I know this Christmas has been a little rocky, even for the Simpsons, but I think these gifts born of thought and love will restore the balance of prajna and samsara. I mean holly and jolly.
jokes.punchline: Catches herself using Buddhist terms, translates to Christmas language
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Lisa's spiritual side and self-correction

jokes.text: Radishes?! It's the most hated part of salad, which is the most hated part of dinner. Radishes, nothing but radishes.
jokes.punchline: Escalating hatred: radishes → salad → dinner
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with perfect Homer food hatred

jokes.text: I'm saving them from the struggle of life.
jokes.punchline: Justifying destruction as mercy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor disguised as philosophy - classic Homer rationalization

jokes.text: What? Lis, you're smart. Why would you give me a book? / 'Cause it's easy to wrap? / Nothing is easy to wrap. I have trouble with scissors.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to admitting basic tool incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's practical limitations

jokes.text: Marge, the brochure promised us a happy Christmas. / My uncle died in your bed. / Merry Christmas.
jokes.punchline: Death revelation delivered as casual Christmas greeting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect deadpan delivery and economy

jokes.text: Shut up, Flanders.
jokes.setup: Flanders trying to explain true meaning of Christmas
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons dismissal of Flanders, economical but expected

jokes.text: Did you give it to me because you thought I'd like it or because you wanted to feel better about yourself?
jokes.setup: Lisa's anger about Bart burning the book
jokes.punchline: Bart's surprisingly insightful question about gift-giving motives
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly profound observation from Bart about altruism vs. self-satisfaction

jokes.text: If you smell your farts in a dream, you die?
jokes.punchline: Bart's completely wrong guess about what he was right about
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart non-sequitur combining bathroom humor with dream mythology

jokes.text: But how'd you afford it? / I sold the gift you gave me. / Get the twist? Obviously.
jokes.punchline: Reveals selling Lisa's gift, dismisses obviousness of irony
jokes.explanation: Good ironic resolution with Bart's casual dismissal of narrative device

jokes.text: No second verses. They creep me out. / Okay, we'll sing a different one. / Oh, that one creeps me out from the beginning.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to rejecting entire song from start
jokes.explanation: Good callback and escalation of Homer's carol phobia

tmdb_overview: Springfield is inundated with holiday revelers after the nuclear power plant springs a radiation leak, causing snow to fall all over town. When the tourists drive up Christmas shopping prices, Marge opens up the Simpsons' house to boarders.
